abstract_ts: In today's rapidly evolving organizations, characterized by increased diversity driven by immigration, technological advancements, and globalization, organizations face the challenge of effectively managing pluralistic environments. This necessitates the expansion of innovative competencies and talent across individual, managerial, as well as organizational levels to maintain competitiveness. Cultural intelligence (CI) emerges as a critical concept for fostering better integration of diversity and promoting competency in diverse teams. This paper explores the role of CI in facilitating diversity integration and managing competencies within pluralistic workplaces. Drawing from a comprehensive review spanning from 2012 to 2023, encompassing 22 selected papers, the study presents insights into the nuanced relationship between CI, diversity integration, and competency development. Additionally, it introduces a theoretical model to clarify how cultural intelligence (CI) affects the integration of competencies and diversity within the Circular HR framework. By synthesizing literature and the author's perspective about pluralistic teams, this work provides practical suggestions for managers with the objective of enhancing team development and management policies. Competency and diversity remain key to the realization of excellence in organizations, and this research provides important input for how organizations can handle current challenges toward the opening of an era of Circular HR.

abstract_ts: Donald Trump’s “America First” foreign policy was explicitly framed as a shift toward national sovereignty and economic protectionism, but the forces that shaped its trajectory remain a subject of debate among scholars and policymakers. This chapter examines how economic, social, and historical factors influenced the development of this policy, even though these underlying dynamics were often overshadowed by political rhetoric. We explore the long-standing relationship between the United States and European powers, tracing how American foreign policy evolved to establish the nation as a global leader. Historically, the United States engaged in multilateral diplomacy and international alliances, but Trump’s America First policy marked a departure from those traditions, prioritizing bilateral trade deals, deregulation, and a reassertion of US economic dominance. America First was central to Trump’s broader Make America Great Again (MAGA) movement, rooted in a vision of restoring past US influence and authority. By moving away from established diplomatic frameworks, the policy signaled a reversal in America’s approach to global leadership. This chapter assesses the implications of that reversal, questioning whether it redefined US strategic priorities or merely reinforced a more transactional and isolationist stance in international relations.

abstract_ts: Donald Trump’s second inauguration set the ideological framework for his administration, emphasizing themes of nationalism, economic strength, and government restructuring. His inaugural address focused on securing American sovereignty, revitalizing domestic industries, and challenging global agreements he viewed as unfavorable. Through strategic rhetoric, Trump positioned his leadership as a turning point for governance, promising swift action on immigration, deregulation, and executive authority. His first-day executive orders reinforced these themes, signaling an aggressive approach to policy implementation. Immigration enforcement was tightened, federal regulations were rolled back, and government operations were streamlined to align with his political vision. Supporters praised these moves as necessary steps toward restoring stability, while critics expressed concerns over power consolidation and the potential erosion of democratic norms. This analysis explores the intersection of Trump’s rhetoric and policy decisions, assessing how his leadership style shaped political discourse and governance in his second term. By examining these early actions, this study provides insight into the broader ideological shifts that influenced national and international dynamics. Understanding this relationship offers valuable perspectives on the future of executive leadership, public engagement, and institutional resilience in the evolving political landscape.

abstract_ts: Focusing on classroom practice, this chapter translates theory into actionable strategies. It outlines lesson planning based on pre-task, during-task and post-task phases, emphasising short, clear instructions, visual cues and cooperative activities. Detailed examples—such as “Simon Says” for vocabulary reinforcement and a “Treasure Hunt Fitness Circuit” linking language prompts with physical stations—illustrate how to integrate language and movement. Differentiation is addressed by proposing multi-level tasks, flexible grouping and scaffolding to accommodate varied language proficiency and special educational needs. The chapter also explores technology’s role: video feedback, augmented and virtual reality, mobile apps and wearables enhance self-assessment and motivation, while digital portfolios document progress. By pairing sample activities with guidelines on inclusion and technological integration, this chapter equips teachers with concrete tools for implementing CLIL-PE.

abstract_ts: This study examined the transformative influence of collaborative teaching and learning methods from the perspective of a university lecturer, providing an in-depth analysis of the complex benefits and obstacles faced when implementing such methodologies in higher education. Drawing on the lecturer’s personal experiences, the study provides a comprehensive overview of the adoption of interdisciplinary team teaching, co-teaching models, and the use of technology-enhanced collaborative platforms, with the goal of creating a more engaging, interactive and efficient learning environment. Significant findings arose from reflective journals, demonstrating not just increased involvement but also the development of important soft skills such as critical thinking, communication and teamwork. The study discussed the logistical and pedagogical issues encountered, such as the need for more resources, effective time management and adaptability to contemporary teaching paradigms, and portrays them as chances for growth and progress rather than impractical obstacles. Finally, the study suggests that collaborative teaching and learning approaches considerably improve the educational experience, creating an academic environment that reflects the collaborative and interdisciplinary nature of today’s workforce. Recommendations are made for educators who want to include these techniques in their practice, emphasising the significance of institutional support; ongoing professional development; and cultivating a learning culture that values and fosters collective knowledge and exploration.

abstract_ts: The little understanding of mining-induced coal face behaviour under near-seam and thick strong strata embedded with pre-existing discrete fractures is addressed in this study. The deeper understanding of longwall stress distribution and resultant face behaviour (deformation, failure, spall, fall) are reliably achieved through panel-scale numerical simulations with realistic incorporation of discrete fractures in not only coal seam but also main roof strata. The study better characterises the shape of stress release zone, which should be “pear” rather than “elliptical” shape. The stress change in direction and magnitude is extensively demonstrated from coal seam to surrounding rocks. The front abutment stress is found up to 2.88 times the pre-mining stress and located 24 m ahead of coal face. The compressive stress is confirmed to re-develop in the reconsolidated rocks to its pre-mining value and can be greater in places. The study reveals that the coal face is more stable when the strength and elastic modulus of main roof rock increase. Similar face behaviour is manifested when the distance between coal seam and main roof increases. In contrast, the face is found to be less stable when the thickness and fracture density of main roof increase. Behind shield support, the top coal behaviour shows clear relationships with main roof rock strength, main roof strata thickness, and distance between main roof and seam floor. The study’s findings help engineers in better understanding longwall coal face behaviour in less favourable geo-mining conditions from which safe and environmentally sustainable coal mining can be better maintained.

abstract_ts: Im Zuge der Energiewende verändert sich das deutsche Stromsystem grundlegend. Um die zunehmenden Schwankungen der wetter- und tageszeitabhängigen Einspeisung erneuerbarer Energien auszugleichen, gewinnt neben dem energieflexiblen Laden und Betrieb von Wärmepumpen im Niederspannungsnetz auf den höheren Spannungsebenen insbesondere die schnelle und umfassende Flexibilisierung der gewerblichen und industriellen Stromnachfrage an Bedeutung. Dazu müssen jedoch die regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen die Wirtschaftlichkeit von Investitionen in Energieflexibilität sicherstellen. In diesem Zusammenhang spielen vor allem individuelle Netzentgelte eine wichtige Rolle. Eine sowohl dem Gewerbe als auch der Industrie zugängliche Form der Sondernetzentgelte ist die atypische Netznutzung nach § 19 Abs. 2 Satz 1 Stromnetzentgeltverordnung. Zentrale Voraussetzung hierfür ist die erhebliche Reduktion des Netzbezugs eines Letztverbrauchers in Hochlastzeitfenstern. Allerdings schränkt die aktuelle Ausgestaltung der atypischen Netznutzung und die Berechnungssystematik der Hochlastzeitfenster zunehmend den Einsatz gewerblicher und industrieller Energieflexibilität ein – obwohl ein solches Verhalten aus marktlicher und systemischer Perspektive mit Vorteilen verbunden wäre. Vor diesem Hintergrund beleuchten wir im vorliegenden Artikel die mit der gegenwärtigen Ausgestaltung der atypischen Netznutzung verbundenen Herausforderungen und präsentieren darauf aufbauend drei konkrete Weiterentwicklungsoptionen, um diese zu adressieren. Bereits kurzfristig umsetzbar ist die Ausweisung von Hochlastzeitfenstern auf monatlicher Basis, wodurch gegenüber dem Status quo der Einfluss einzelner Tage bei der Ermittlung der Hochlastzeitfenster reduziert wird. Durch die kurz- bis mittelfristige Erweiterung einer Spannungsebenenübergreifenden Residuallastbetrachtung sowie einer langfristig einführbaren Ergänzung tagesaktueller Bestätigungen bzw. Aufhebungen von Hochlastzeitfenstern könnte die Systemdienlichkeit sukzessive weiter gesteigert werden. Die Vorteile des kurzfristigen Reformvorschlags werden abschließend anhand einer Fallstudie der bivalenten Prozessdampferzeugung in der Papierindustrie veranschaulicht und diskutiert.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a hybridization of Multilayer Neural Network-based Bangla phoneme recognition method for Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) incorporating dynamic parameters. The method consists of four stages: at first stage, a multilayer neural network (MLN) converts acoustic features, mel frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs), into phoneme probabilities. Phoneme probabilities from the first stage are inserted into second stage MLN for obtaining more accurate phoneme probabilities with reduced context where the third stage computes dynamic (velocity (A) and acceleration (AA)) parameters from the phoneme probabilities by using three point linear regressions (LRs). Finally, the phoneme probabilities, dynamic parameters, A and AA, and the input MFCCs, combined as hybrid features, are fed into a hidden Markov model (HMM) based classifier to obtain more accurate phoneme strings. From the experiments on Bangla speech corpus prepared by us, it is observed that the proposed method provides higher phoneme recognition performance than the existing method. Moreover, it requires a fewer mixture components in the HMMs.

abstract_ts: Characteristics of carbonyl compounds including their concentrations, possible sources and personal exposure were investigated in six underground subway stations in Shanghai in June 2008. Nineteen out of twenty target carbonyls were detected with the exception of 2,5-dimethylbenzaldehyd. Formaldehyde was the most abundant carbonyl, followed by acetaldehyde and acetone. Their highest mean concentrations were 31.5±2.96, 22.5±2.75 and 18.7±2.69 μg/m3, and contributed more than 30%, 21% and 18% of the total carbonyl concentrations, respectively. A significant correlation (r>0.5, p<0.01) between the concentration of the low molecular-weight carbonyl compounds (<C5) and ozone was found in the subway stations. Comparison of the indoor and outdoor carbonyl concentrations found "indoor>outdoor" for every carbonyl species except for benzaldehyde and o/m/p-tolualdehyde which might possible come from the outdoor source as a result of air exchanged by the ventilation system. The personal exposure showed that the underground subway stations were an important microenvironment for exposure to formaldehyde and acetaldehyde, especially for people who worked in the subway stations.

abstract_ts: Urbanization and deforestation are leading to quick forest loss, which is mounting enormous risks to environmental sustainability as well as climate stability. That is why monitoring and estimating the coverage of trees is important for successful conservation purposes. This project proposes a tree count prediction and deforestation detection approach with Sentinel-2 satellite images, based on the images dataset. This project employs a U-Net convolutional neural network (CNN) for semantic segmentation and couples it with NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index)-based vegetation analysis to identify tree canopy and determine the tree count accurately. The model identifies tree areas using spectral information from satellite images, processes binary mask images using morphological operations, and computes tree counts. Comparative study of the model with models such as SFA-Net reveals the U-Net model's greater precision, speed, and accuracy under different environmental and geographical contexts. This project indicates the promise of deep learning and machine learning in large-scale environmental monitoring by proposing a lightweight tool to tackle the issues of tree loss and deforestation.

abstract_ts: This research proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM model based on a multi-task learning (MTL) approach to improve pronunciation and grammar training in computer-assisted language learning (CALL) systems. The CNN extracts meaningful acoustic features for pronunciation analysis, whereas the LSTM models sequential dependencies for correcting grammar. This hybrid approach is specifically adapted to process both temporal speech patterns and syntactic structures concomitantly. A multilingual dataset consisting of 2,140 speakers from 177 countries and 214 native languages was employed. The model surpasses baseline Transformer and LSTM-only models with a BLEU score of 0.85, 91.7% grammar accuracy, and 89.5% sentence fluency. Pronunciation evaluation measures are 90.1% phoneme classification accuracy, 88.9% pronunciation score per word, and 87.6% fluency score. A common MTL loss function allows the model to give reliable real-time feedback on both tasks. The outcomes illustrate the competence of the model and the practical utility of deep learning-based CALL tools for international language learners.

abstract_ts: Early and accurate diagnosis is important when treating breast cancer. Amongst approaches, conventional imaging methods such as mammography and MRI have their limitations handling dense breast tissues. In this review, the topic of discussion is ML-based approaches to identifying breast cancers based on SVM, Random Forests, CNNs, XGBoost, and hybrid CNN-SVM approaches. Transfer learning and federated learning have been debated, with recent use concentrating on their improvement issues of effectiveness and privacy protection. Each approach is compared to the BreakHis, WDBC, and CBIS-DDSM datasets and an accuracy matrix of accuracy, precision, sensitivity, and AUC-ROC. Ensemble and hybrid techniques are superior to the conventional algorithm with accuracies of <tex>$\text{9 7 \%}$</tex>. Explanation of predictions generated by the participating tools using XAI techniques such as SHAP and Grad-CAM has raised their acceptance within the clinics. Data imbalance, high computational cost, and explainability are some issues with existing developments. models. This review then establishes ML's growing presence in reliable automated breast cancer diagnosis.

abstract_ts: One of the most dangerous diseases and a leading cause of death worldwide is skin cancer. Fatalities can be significantly reduced if skin cancer is detected and diagnosed prior to the initial event. However, skin cancer is primarily identified through visual inspection, which can be less accurate. To improve this, deep learning methods have been put forth to help dermatologists diagnose skin malignancies more accurately and early. This paper aims to develop a reliable model for detecting skin cancer using advanced deep learning techniques. The model uses the CNN architecture and also the best architecture among EfficientNet, VGG19, Inception, Xception, ResNet, which have been trained on the large ISIC skin cancer dataset from Kaggle containing 1140 benign lesion images and 1197 malignant lesion images. After evaluating these individual models, an ensemble model will be constructed to combine the strengths of CNN and the best-performing pretrained architecture to classify skin lesions. The goal is to achieve high precision in classifying skin lesions as malignant (cancerous) or benign (noncancerous), improving early detection and treatment. The process consists of the following crucial steps: acquisition of images, data preprocessing, extraction of features, and classification. The final output is a model that, when given an image of a skin lesion, analyzes it and classifies it as either malignant or benign. This provides a valuable tool for the early and accurate identification of skin cancer.

abstract_ts: A common issue generally facing by people in solar panel installation is dust buildup. This directly effects on lowering down efficiency, also reduces energy output, and thus increases maintenance needs. Solar energy is found to be a key part of this global shift to sustainable energy sources. To address this issue, we have present a deep learning model for the automatic detection of dust. The model was trained using high-resolution annotated images from the Maharashtra Remote Sensing Application Centre (MRSAC) with the help of Roboflow platform. The model uses object detection techniques and it has achieved a mean Average Precision (mAP) of <tex>$\mathbf{9 9. 5 \%}$</tex> along with the perfect precision and recall scores. Due to its high accuracy false detections minimizes and allows for accurate identification of dusty surfaces. This makes very suitable for our real world solar monitoring applications. This technology is much more efficient and it supports scheduling of maintenance, which may lead to lower down the long-term operating expenses by allowing for early analysis. There is also a potential to integrate the model with (Internet of things) IoT-based systems and predictive maintenance strategies, which can enhance the performance and also the lifespan of solar PV systems. The future scope of the research will focus on improving the model's reliability in different environmental conditions, implementing it in real-time through edge computing platforms, and investigating its possible use in robotic cleaning systems. The effort pushes the sustainable development of automated technologies for managing solar energy systems and optimizing renewable energy production.

abstract_ts: Traditional search engines often struggle to deliver accurate, up-to-date information, particularly for rapidly evolving topics, due to their reliance on semi-structured databases that can become stale. To address these limitations, we propose an enhanced search engine architecture leveraging Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology. Our system combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with sophisticated document retrieval mechanisms to deliver contextually relevant and current information to users. The proposed framework's key innovation lies in its dynamic document retrieval system that continuously updates through databases, web crawlers, and APIs, enabling the LLM to generate responses based on fresh data. This approach significantly reduces the likelihood of inaccuracies and hallucinations commonly associated with standalone llMs. We further enhance the model's precision through domain-specific fine-tuning across sectors like finance, healthcare, and news media, optimizing both accuracy and relevance for specialized queries. A distinctive feature of our system is its transparent approach to information delivery, presenting users with both generated answers and their supporting source documents.The framework integrates private institutional data with publicly available knowledge to enhance response generation. This dual-output mechanism builds user trust by providing verifiable context for all responses. Through extensive testing, our model is demonstrated to have superior performance in delivering domain-specific, current, and accurate search results compared to traditional search engines.

abstract_ts: Monolithic gamma-ray detectors can be used in single photon emission computed tomography systems for monitoring the delivered dose during boron neutron capture therapy treatments. Gamma-ray hit localization in thick monolithic scintillator crystals is a challenging task due to internal reflections and Compton scattering. Existing methods like the center of gravity (CoG) are susceptible to reconstruction uncertainties at the crystal edges, while approaches, including nonlinear analytical and statistical models, such as the maximum-likelihood, require significant computational resources. Artificial neural networks (ANNs) offer significant improvements in terms of accuracy and computational speed. In this study, we develop a supervised ANN regression algorithm for real-time position reconstruction in a thick square lanthanum bromide crystal [LaBr<inline-formula> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{3}(\text {Ce}+\text {Sr})$ </tex-math></inline-formula>] with <inline-formula> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5\, \text {cm}\times 5\,\text {cm}\times 2\,\text {cm}$ </tex-math></inline-formula> dimensions, coupled with an <inline-formula> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times 8$ </tex-math></inline-formula> matrix of silicon photomultipliers. The implemented neural network was trained and tested using calibration data acquired irradiating the crystal with a collimated 137Cs source (pencil-beam irradiation). The detector in combination with the ANN model achieves a positioning accuracy for single-gamma-ray events of approximately 2.6 mm in the central region, evaluated as the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of the prediction error distribution, slightly worsening toward the edges. The imaging capabilities of the detector in combination with a channel-edge pinhole collimator were then evaluated by acquiring images of a movable uncollimated 137Cs point source. The source was shifted in nine different positions at 3 mm distance from each other and the resolution of the system was evaluated fitting the images with a Gaussian curve. An image spatial resolution of around 8 mm FWHM was obtained, dominated as expected by the collimator geometry, with an accuracy of 0.7 mm in estimating the position of the point source.

abstract_ts: Carpenters in construction commonly perform overhead work tasks such as installing ceiling panels, plastering, and formwork. While laboratory studies suggest exoskeletons may reduce strain during such work, their effectiveness in real-life conditions remains uncertain. This randomized cross-over field study investigates the effects of a passive shoulder exoskeleton on muscle activity and kinematics in 20 carpenters during overhead work tasks. Results showed reduced peak muscle activity (nEMG) in the shoulders and forearms (-3.1 to -9.4 %), but increased activity in the lower back (2.3-3.4 %) when using the exoskeleton. Kinematic analysis revealed minimal changes in range-of-motion (<3.1°) and peak joint angles. Participants reported good usability and willingness to use the exoskeleton in the future. This study demonstrates positive effects on shoulder load in real working conditions but highlights the need to consider increased low-back load when implementing exoskeletons for overhead work.

abstract_ts: We evaluated how the Severe Acute Respiratory disease from Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) epidemic impacted transfusion services, transfusion support required by Covid-19 patients and their clinical outcome. In Italy, the first confirmed case of SARS-CoV-2 infection was registered on 21 February 2020. As of 20 April, about 250 000 cases were registered, 1143 of which were in the province of Pescara. We compared transfusion services provided by the blood centre of Pescara between 1 March and 20 April 2019 and between 1 March and 20 April 2020. We assessed the number and type of blood components donated, those transfused in the various hospital departments and those transfused to Covid-19 patients. Compared to 2019, we documented a decrease of 32% in the number of donations. The number of transfusions increased by 139% in the infectious diseases department (IDD), dedicated to Covid-19 patients, and by 76% in the intensive care unit (ICU), whereas it markedly decreased in the other departments. Of 299 patients with Covid-19, 60 were transfused (20.1%). Transfused patients in the ICU were significantly younger than those in IDD and had a lower number of lymphocytes, lower post-transfusion increment of haemoglobin levels and higher D-dimer and C reactive protein values. Mortality rate was 60.7% among transfused patients in the ICU and 39.0% among those in the IDD (p = 0.02). The Covid-19 epidemic had a profound impact on transfusion activities. The important blood demand for Covid-19 patients was satisfied because of the reduction in activities in other hospital wards. Covid-19-positive transfused patients showed a very poor prognosis.

abstract_ts: The aim of the study was to assess the effects of computerized provider order entry (CPOE) for parenteral nutrition (PN) on selected clinical parameters of extremely low-birth-weight (ELBW) neonates in a tertiary neonatal intensive care unit. This study was a retrospective data analysis of preterm infants born over a 1-year period before and after introduction of CPOE. PN composition during the first 2 weeks of life, change in weight, head circumference and body length z-scores were assessed. Duration of PN and selected neonatal complications were compared. Logistic regression was used to adjust for confounding variables (sex, birth weight, and birth-gestational age). Ninety-four patients in 2 equal groups were analyzed. Birth weight (median 810 vs 825 g) and gestational age (27 vs 27 weeks) were comparable. After adjustment for confounding variables, on the first and seventh day of life, significantly more energy (P < .001), protein (P < .001), lipid (P < .03), and carbohydrate (P < .02) were administered in the CPOE group than in the manually calculated (MC) prescriptions group. The CPOE group had lower weight loss (P < .001), a significant improvement in linear growth, and faster regain of birth weight (P < .01) compared with the MC group. The CPOE positively influences the quality of PN in ELBW infants. It also significantly reduced initial weight loss, time to regain birth weight, and linear growth. It may also have beneficial effects on selected clinical outcomes, which requires further study.
